perturbation.original_text: 5. TERM This Agreement will be effective as of the Effective Date. Unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, the initial term of this Agreement ("Initial Term") will be ten (10) years from the Effective Date, provided that at any time following the 7th anniversary of such date, either party may terminate such strategic alliance upon not less than 60 days' prior written notice to the other party. After the Initial Term, this Agreement may only be renewed if authorized officers of ARGO and YEC agree in writing at least thirty (30) days before the expiration of the Initial Term or any renewal term to a renewal, including the period of the renewal term. "Term" means the Initial Term and any such renewal term. The parties may terminate this Agreement during the Term as follows: (a) Termination by either party. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving written notice of termination to the other party, which termination will be effective immediately upon such notice, if the other party defaults in the performance of any of its material obligations provided for in this Agreement and fails to cure such default within sixty (60) days after receipt of notice from the other party of such default, unless a plan for remedying such default has been proposed by the defaulting party and accepted by the non- defaulting party within such period. (b) Termination by both parties. ARGO and YEC may terminate this Agreement at any point provided that both parties agree in writingto such a termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. TERM This Agreement will be effective as of the Effective Date. Unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, the initial term of this Agreement ("Initial Term") will be ten (10) years from the Effective Date, provided that at any time following the 7th anniversary of such date, either party may terminate such strategic alliance upon not less than 60 days' prior written notice to the other party. After the Initial Term, this Agreement may only be renewed if authorized officers of ARGO and YEC agree in writing at least thirty (30) days before the expiration of the Initial Term or any renewal term to a renewal, including the period of the renewal term. "Term" means the Initial Term and any such renewal term. The parties may terminate this Agreement during the Term as follows: (a) Termination by either party. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving written notice of termination to the other party, which termination will be effective immediately upon such notice. (b) Termination by both parties. ARGO and YEC may terminate this Agreement at any point provided that both parties agree in writingto such a termination.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the clause that requires a defaulting party to be given 60 days to remedy a breach before the non-defaulting party can terminate the agreement in section 5(a). Now Section 5(a) Termination by either party states the termination will be effective immediately upon such notice
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. TERM This Agreement will be effective as of the Effective Date. Unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, the initial term of this Agreement ("Initial Term") will be ten (10) years from the Effective Date, provided that at any time following the 7th anniversary of such date, either party may terminate such strategic alliance upon not less than 60 days' prior written notice to the other party. After the Initial Term, this Agreement may only be renewed if authorized officers of ARGO and YEC agree in writing at least thirty (30) days before the expiration of the Initial Term or any renewal term to a renewal, including the period of the renewal term. "Term" means the Initial Term and any such renewal term. The parties may terminate this Agreement during the Term as follows: (a) Termination by either party. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving written notice of termination to the other party, which termination will be effective immediately upon such notice. (b) Termination by both parties. ARGO and YEC may terminate this Agreement at any point provided that both parties agree in writingto such a termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 60-day cure period for defaults, as originally stated in the original text's Section 5(a). The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, now states in Section 5(a) that termination is effective immediately upon notice. This directly contradicts the original requirement for a 60-day cure period, creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding the termination process, and fitting under the Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction perturbation definition.

perturbation.original_text: 8. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY; CO-DEVELOPMENT, IMPROVEMENT, MODIFICATION This agreement does not give either party any rights, title or interest in the other party's trade name, trademarks, copyrights, patents, trade secrets, know-how, proprietary data, confidential information, or other intellectual property (hereinafter collectively "Intellectual Property"). Except as expressly stipulated in this Agreement, each party shall not without any prior written consent, use, copy, modify or license the other party's Intellectual Properties supplied pursuant to this Agreement. Unless otherwise agreed between the parties or stipulated in a separate related agreement such as the SPA or DA, each party confirms and agrees that any Intellectual Properties are hereby supplied to the other party on an "as is" basis. There are no warranties by either party with respect to such Intellectual Properties, express or implied including the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose and non-infringement. In the event that the parties mutually agree to explore jointly in any manner, design and/or develop new products or improve or modify ARGO's current products, the parties will negotiate, in good faith, in an attempt to conclude one or more appropriate license agreements prior to either party's use of the Intellectual Property of the other.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY; CO-DEVELOPMENT, IMPROVEMENT, MODIFICATION This agreement does not give either party any rights, title or interest in the other party's trade name, trademarks, copyrights, patents, trade secrets, know-how, proprietary data, confidential information, or other intellectual property (hereinafter collectively "Intellectual Property"), except that YEC shall have a non-exclusive license to use ARGO's Intellectual Property for the purpose of marketing and distribution. Except as expressly stipulated in this Agreement, each party shall not without any prior written consent, use, copy, modify or license the other party's Intellectual Properties supplied pursuant to this Agreement. Unless otherwise agreed between the parties or stipulated in a separate related agreement such as the SPA or DA, each party confirms and agrees that any Intellectual Properties are hereby supplied to the other party on an "as is" basis. There are no warranties by either party with respect to such Intellectual Properties, express or implied including the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose and non-infringement. In the event that the parties mutually agree to explore jointly in any manner, design and/or develop new products or improve or modify ARGO's current products, the parties will negotiate, in good faith, in an attempt to conclude one or more appropriate license agreements prior to either party's use of the Intellectual Property of the other.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 8 introduces an in-text contradiction. Initially, the agreement states that neither party gains any rights to the other's intellectual property. However, the modified version grants YEC a non-exclusive license to use ARGO's IP for marketing and distribution. This directly contradicts the original statement that 'This agreement does not give either party any rights, title or interest in the other party's intellectual property'. This creates uncertainty about the extent of IP rights and usage permissions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY; CO-DEVELOPMENT, IMPROVEMENT, MODIFICATION This agreement does not give either party any rights, title or interest in the other party's trade name, trademarks, copyrights, patents, trade secrets, know-how, proprietary data, confidential information, or other intellectual property (hereinafter collectively "Intellectual Property"), except that YEC shall have a non-exclusive license to use ARGO's Intellectual Property for the purpose of marketing and distribution. Except as expressly stipulated in this Agreement, each party shall not without any prior written consent, use, copy, modify or license the other party's Intellectual Properties supplied pursuant to this Agreement. Unless otherwise agreed between the parties or stipulated in a separate related agreement such as the SPA or DA, each party confirms and agrees that any Intellectual Properties are hereby supplied to the other party on an "as is" basis. There are no warranties by either party with respect to such Intellectual Properties, express or implied including the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose and non-infringement. In the event that the parties mutually agree to explore jointly in any manner, design and/or develop new products or improve or modify ARGO's current products, the parties will negotiate, in good faith, in an attempt to conclude one or more appropriate license agreements prior to either party's use of the Intellectual Property of the other.
perturbation.justification: The original text states, 'This agreement does not give either party any rights, title or interest in the other party's intellectual property.' The changed text modifies this statement by adding 'except that YEC shall have a non-exclusive license to use ARGO's Intellectual Property for the purpose of marketing and distribution.' This addition directly contradicts the initial statement, as it explicitly grants YEC a right (a non-exclusive license) to ARGO's intellectual property, which was previously denied. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, but the contradiction arises because the original statement is now false within the modified agreement, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12.5. Applicable Law This agreement will be governed by and must be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Israel. All disputes arising pursuant to this Agreement shall be exclusively brought in the courts of competent jurisdiction residing in Tel Aviv, Israel.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.5. Applicable Law This agreement will be governed by and must be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Israel, except for matters related to intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of Japan. All disputes arising pursuant to this Agreement shall be exclusively brought in the courts of competent jurisdiction residing in Tel Aviv, Israel, except for disputes concerning intellectual property which will be brought in the courts of competent jurisdiction residing in Tokyo, Japan.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction within section 12.5 by creating an exception to the governing law and jurisdiction. The first sentence states that the agreement is governed by the laws of Israel and disputes will be handled in Tel Aviv courts. However, the added clause specifies that intellectual property matters will be governed by Japanese law and handled in Tokyo courts. This contradicts the initially stated exclusive jurisdiction and applicable law, potentially leading to confusion and disputes over which legal framework applies to specific issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.5. Applicable Law This agreement will be governed by and must be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Israel, except for matters related to intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of Japan. All disputes arising pursuant to this Agreement shall be exclusively brought in the courts of competent jurisdiction residing in Tel Aviv, Israel, except for disputes concerning intellectual property which will be brought in the courts of competent jurisdiction residing in Tokyo, Japan.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear in-text contradiction. The original text states that all disputes are to be brought in Tel Aviv, Israel. The changed text modifies this by stating that intellectual property disputes will be brought in Tokyo, Japan. This creates a direct conflict as to which jurisdiction applies to intellectual property disputes, thus creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies' perturbation type. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, reinforcing the existence of the contradiction within the document itself.
